title: quercus oleoides
intro: Quercus oleoides, also known as the Evergreen Oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayan regions of India and Nepal. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus oleoides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural remedy for various skin conditions. Quercus oleoides is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed in the form of tea, capsules, or topical creams, providing a range of health benefits to those who use it.
